content:
Generate a concise commit message for this diff. The commit wraps pace A-AAw. Output only the message, no quotes.
diff --git a/Tools/buk/bujb_cli.sh b/Tools/buk/bujb_cli.sh
index a569176f..703c782b 100755
--- a/Tools/buk/bujb_cli.sh
+++ b/Tools/buk/bujb_cli.sh
@@ -125,6 +125,20 @@ bujb_fenestrate_command() {
   bujb_fenestrate
 }
 
+# bujb_privileged_ssh_command - run an arbitrary command as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER.
+# Pass-through: argv joined into ssh's remote-command position; no shell wrapping.
+bujb_privileged_ssh_command() {
+  buc_doc_brief "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"
+  buc_doc_param "command..." "Command tokens executed on the remote node (pass-through)"
+  buc_doc_shown || return 0
+
+  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
+  test $# -ge 1            || buc_usage_die
+
+  bujb_resolve_investiture
+  bujb_privileged_ssh "$@"
+}
+
 # bujb_garrison_bash - garrison ceremony, native bash workload shell (Linux/Mac).
 bujb_garrison_bash() {
   buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_linux/bubep_mac node (shell-letter b)"
diff --git a/Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh b/Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh
index e27f5b8d..9dba4ae0 100644
--- a/Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh
+++ b/Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh
@@ -814,4 +814,34 @@ bujb_fenestrate() {
   buc_step "Fenestrate succeeded"
 }
 
+######################################################################
+# Public: Privileged SSH (thin admin-side pass-through)
+
+# bujb_privileged_ssh COMMAND... -- run an arbitrary command on the BURN
+# node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER under key-only auth. Pass-through: argv is
+# handed to ssh as the remote command without shell wrapping (operator
+# prepends `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, etc. as the platform requires).
+# Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand. Returns
+# ssh's exit code.
+bujb_privileged_ssh() {
+  zbujb_sentinel
+  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
+    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
+  test $# -ge 1 \
+    || buc_die "bujb_privileged_ssh: command required"
+
+  buc_step "Privileged SSH: ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"
+
+  local z_exit=0
+  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
+      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
+      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
+      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
+      -o ConnectTimeout=15                        \
+      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"      \
+      "$@"                                        \
+    || z_exit=$?
+  return "${z_exit}"
+}
+
 # eof
diff --git a/Tools/buk/buwz_zipper.sh b/Tools/buk/buwz_zipper.sh
index 59444800..87882b75 100644
--- a/Tools/buk/buwz_zipper.sh
+++ b/Tools/buk/buwz_zipper.sh
@@ -94,6 +94,9 @@ zbuwz_kindle() {
   # Jurisdiction operational — fenestrate (bujb_cli.sh)
   buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_FENESTRATE       "buw-jpF"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_fenestrate_command" "param1"  "Fenestrate — admin SSH trust + sshd_config harden (Windows)"
 
+  # Jurisdiction operational — privileged SSH pass-through (bujb_cli.sh)
+  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_PRIVILEGED_SSH   "buw-jpS"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_privileged_ssh_command" "param1"  "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"
+
   # Jurisdiction operational — garrison ceremonies (bujb_cli.sh)
   buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_BASH    "buw-jpGb"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_bash"     "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter b: native bash, Linux/Mac)"
   buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_CYGWIN  "buw-jpGc"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_cygwin"   "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter c: Cygwin bash, Windows)"
diff --git a/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc b/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc
index 3106ff33..36e89283 100644
--- a/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc
+++ b/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc
@@ -43,6 +43,7 @@
 
 // Jurisdiction Tabtargets
 :bust_fenestrate:                  <<bust_fenestrate,Fenestrate>>
+:bust_privileged_ssh:              <<bust_privileged_ssh,Privileged SSH>>
 :bust_garrison_bash:               <<bust_garrison_bash,Garrison Bash>>
 :bust_garrison_cygwin:             <<bust_garrison_cygwin,Garrison Cygwin>>
 :bust_garrison_wsl:                <<bust_garrison_wsl,Garrison WSL>>
@@ -1584,8 +1585,8 @@ operator manages sshd directly.
 
 === Tabtarget Catalog
 
-Fourteen tabtargets cover regime configuration, fenestrate, garrison
-ceremony, and workload dispatch.
+Fifteen tabtargets cover regime configuration, fenestrate, privileged
+SSH, garrison ceremony, and workload dispatch.
 PowerShell garrison and dispatch are deferred and not enumerated below.
 
 ==== Regime Configuration
@@ -1640,6 +1641,24 @@ Validate a single {burp_regime} profile
 on a Windows OpenSSH node (uniform; no shell-letter variant).
 Capital `F` marks this as a persistent-config-application tabtarget.
 
+==== Privileged SSH
+
+[[bust_privileged_ssh]]
+//axvo_tabtarget
+{bust_privileged_ssh}::
+`buw-jpS` —
+Thin admin-side pass-through that runs an arbitrary command on a
+node as {burp_privileged_user} under key-only auth.
+Pass-through: argv after the {burp_investiture} is handed to ssh
+as the remote command without shell wrapping; the operator
+prepends `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, or whatever the
+target platform requires.
+Scope spans every {burn_platform} — Linux/Mac admin trust comes
+from operator-managed `ssh-copy-id`, Windows admin trust from
+{busn_fenestrate}.
+Capital `S` marks the admin-side scope (privileged user) and
+distinguishes it from `buw-jws` (workload interactive session).
+
 ==== Garrison (per shell-letter)
 
 [[bust_garrison_bash]]
@@ -1809,6 +1828,8 @@ include::BUSTPV-PrivilegeRegimeValidate.adoc[]
 
 include::BUSJPF-Fenestrate.adoc[]
 
+include::BUSJPS-PrivilegedSsh.adoc[]
+
 include::BUSJGB-GarrisonBash.adoc[]
 
 include::BUSJGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc[]
diff --git a/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJPS-PrivilegedSsh.adoc b/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJPS-PrivilegedSsh.adoc
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..ce47272c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJPS-PrivilegedSsh.adoc
@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@
+//axhob_operation
+{bust_privileged_ssh}
+
+NOTE: Thin admin-side pass-through that runs an arbitrary command on a
+node as {burp_privileged_user} under key-only auth. The remote command
+position is handed to ssh verbatim — no shell wrapping by BUK; the
+operator prepends `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, or whatever the
+target platform requires. Scope: any {burp_investiture}, regardless of
+{burn_platform}; admin trust on Linux/Mac comes from operator-managed
+`ssh-copy-id`, on Windows from {busn_fenestrate}, and the privileged-key
+auth path surfaces success or failure uniformly without platform
+branching at this layer.
+
+//axhopt_typed_parameter
+{burp_investiture}
+
+//axhopt_typed_parameter
+At least one command token after the {burp_investiture}; an empty
+command tail dies with a usage diagnostic.
+
+//axhoq_precondition
+A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
+{burp_privileged_key_file} is currently trusted by the node for
+{burp_privileged_user} (Windows: {busn_fenestrate} has run; Linux/Mac:
+operator has placed the admin pubkey out-of-band).
+
+//axhos_step
+. *Open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} using
+{burp_privileged_key_file}; key-auth only (no password fallback).
+
+//axhos_step
+. *Hand command to ssh*: pass the remaining argv to ssh as the remote
+command position without local wrapping or quoting transformation.
+
+//axhos_step
+. *Propagate exit*: return ssh's exit code as the operation's exit
+code so the remote command's success or failure surfaces unaltered.
+
+//axhog_guarantee
+No persistent state change at this layer — the operation is an
+ephemeral exec; any persistent change is the responsibility of the
+operator-supplied command body.
+
+//axhoc_completion
+Exit 0 when ssh and the remote command both return 0.
+Non-zero on SSH connection or authentication failure, or on a non-zero
+remote exit; the remote exit code is propagated verbatim.
diff --git a/tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh b/tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh
new file mode 100755
index 00000000..5e31c27b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tt/buw-jpS.PrivilegedSsh.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+export BURD_LAUNCHER=".buk/launcher.buw_workbench.sh"
+exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/../${BURD_LAUNCHER}" "${0##*/}" "${@}"

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Add buw-jpS privileged SSH pass-through tabtarget and BUSJPS spec section

lastPrompt: Generate a concise commit message for this diff. The commit wraps pace A-AAw. Output only the message, no quotes. diff --git a/Tools/buk/bujb_cli.sh b/Tools/buk/bujb_cli.sh index a569176f..703c782b 10…
